title: Federal Building and Fire Safety Investigation of the World Trade Center Disaster: Occupant Behavior, Egress, and Emergency Communications
author_or_org: National Institute of Standards and Technology
strengths: Official occupancy and timing synthesis with explicit uncertainty bounds.
limitations: Does not provide a clean civilian-vs-military split or exact women/children death counts for the tower cohort.
notes: Primary denominator source for the WTC tower episode.

title: National Census of Fatal Occupational Injuries in 2001
geographic_scope: World Trade Center and Pentagon worker fatalities
variables_supported: women share of WTC worker fatalities
women share of Pentagon worker fatalities
strengths: Official sex-share tabulation for 9/11 workplace deaths.
limitations: WTC women share covers the broader World Trade Center worker-fatality cohort, not only the NIST tower-occupant cohort.
notes: Used only as a cautious proxy input for WTC women RR and disproportionality.

title: A New View of the Youngest 9/11 Victims and a Stuffed Peter Rabbit
strengths: Institutional source with a clear statement that all eight child victims were on the commandeered aircraft.
notes: Used to preserve source-grounded zero child deaths in non-aircraft episodes.

title: The 9/11 Commission Report
author_or_org: National Commission on Terrorist Attacks Upon the United States
variables_supported: Pentagon civilian and military building deaths
strengths: Canonical official chronology and victim-status split for the Pentagon building.
notes: Used for exact aircraft timing and Pentagon military/civilian building deaths.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
pages_or_sections: FAQ entries on victims, Wedge 1, and Wedge 2 occupancy
strengths: Official memorial source with concise site-specific facts.
limitations: Does not publish a civilian-vs-military denominator split for the affected wedges.
notes: Used for Flight 77 totals and Pentagon affected-wedge occupancy context.

title: Pentagon Renovation Program Report
author_or_org: Washington Headquarters Services / Pentagon Renovation Program
strengths: Program documentation with bounded occupancy figures for the attacked wedges.
limitations: Occupancy is not split by civilian vs military status.
notes: Used to show why the Pentagon is the only plausible DR site but still lacks a publishable status-split denominator.

title: Victims of Flight 77
strengths: Convenient full roster with ages for all innocent passengers and crew.
limitations: Secondary source; adult sex counts require manual coding from names and biographies.
notes: Used for Flight 77 women, children, and adult-male subgroup counts.

title: Crew and Passengers
strengths: Official memorial roster naming all innocent passengers and crew.
limitations: Does not directly tabulate sex counts; adult women counts require manual coding from the roster.

title: The Story of Flight 93
strengths: Official memorial chronology and innocent-person count.
limitations: Uses rounded times rather than exact seconds.
notes: Used for Flight 93 innocent total; exact crash second is taken from the 9/11 Commission chronology.

title: Deora Frances Bodley
strengths: Official page explicitly identifying the youngest person aboard Flight 93 and her age.
limitations: Single-person biography rather than a full table.
notes: Used to preserve the source-grounded zero for children aboard Flight 93.
